post.content:
Given a list of tuples, find the list which belong to same tuple.
Input (Matrix): 
int input[][] = {{1,2},{3,4},{7,2},{5,12},{11,3},{9,7}}

Output:
ArrayList of Arraylist: {{1,2,7,9},{3,4,11},{5,12}}

Note: Order of output is not important.

My approach : I used disjoint sets to solve this question. At the end, group with same parent will belong to same list and then we can output the list. Looking for a better approach (if available).
Thanks!

Similar problem: https://leetcode.com/problems/accounts-merge/
can you clarify? are all tuples length 2 in the input? what do you mean belong to the same tuple?

One more approach can be to create an adjacency list of the tuples (considering a tuple as undirected edge). After that, run dfs on the unvisited nodes and print all the nodes in a single component during DFS runtime.
this is very similar to Account Merge problem - disjoint set is a way to go. Did you pass the screening?

Basically I\'d use the Union Find. This is a standard union find tempalte question. This is just this one, https://leetcode.com/problems/accounts-merge/\n\nUse the index as the key, use the length of input to initialize the UF. After UF union/find, call uf.find(xxx) to get the parent index, and group all entry by the parental index in a hashmap, then convert to bare array.
Union-Find aka Disjoint Set has O(n) complexity (assuming inverse Ackermann is O(1) for real-life scenarios),  therefore you can not do better
